name: ledger-app-clients-ethereum

metadata.name: ledger-app-clients.ethereum
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Python client for the Ledger Ethereum application

This package allows to communicate with the Ledger Ethereum application, either on a
real device, or emulated on Speculos.

## Installation from pypi.org

You can install the client from `pypi.org`:

  ```bash
  pip install ledger_app_clients.ethereum
  ```

## Installation from sources

You can also install the client from this repo:

```bash
pip install .
```

functions.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.keychain._init_key
documentation: Open the corresponding PEM file and load its key in the global dict

functions.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.keychain.sign_data
documentation: Generate a SECP256K1 signature of the given data with the given key

classes.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.keychain.Key
documentation: Private key PEM files have to be named the same (lowercase) as their corresponding enum entries
Example: for an entry in the Enum named DEV, its PEM file must be at keychain/dev.pem

modules.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.__version__
documentation: don't change, don't track in version control

functions.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.utils.normalize_vrs
documentation: eth_account requires it for some reason

modules.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.command_builder
documentation: documentation about APDU format is available here:

functions.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.eip712.InputData.get_array_levels
documentation: From a string typename, extract the type and all the array depth

functions.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.eip712.InputData.get_typesize
documentation: From a string typename, extract the type and its size

functions.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.eip712.InputData.send_filtering_message_info
documentation: ledgerjs doesn't actually sign anything, and instead uses already pre-computed signatures

functions.name: ledger_app_clients.ethereum.eip712.InputData.send_filtering_show_field
documentation: ledgerjs doesn't actually sign anything, and instead uses already pre-computed signatures
